title: Image management tools with API integration
purpose: Provides functions to create, list, get, and delete images via a remote API, handling HTTP requests and response parsing.
module_context: Defines API base URL from environment variables and exports schemas for image operations. Includes helper function to build API URLs with project and session parameters.

methods.name: buildUrl
methods.normalized_params: (path: string, project: string, session: string, extraParams?: Record<string, string>): string

steps.content: Construct a URL using the provided path and base API endpoint.
Set the project parameter in the URL search parameters.
Set the session parameter in the URL search parameters.
If extra parameters are provided, add each key-value pair to the URL search parameters.
Return the final URL string with all parameters included.

methods.name: handleCreateImage
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, name: string, source: string): Promise<CreateImageResult>

steps.content: Send a POST request to the image creation endpoint with the image name and source.
IF the response is not successful, parse the error message and throw an error.
ELSE parse the JSON response to extract the image ID.
Return a success result with the image ID.
Send a GET request to the images listing endpoint.
IF the response is not successful, throw an error with the status text.
ELSE parse the JSON response to extract the images array.
Return a result containing the list of images.

methods.name: handleGetImage
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, id: string): Promise<GetImageResult>

steps.content: Send a GET request to the specific image endpoint using the image ID.
IF the response is not successful, check if it's a 404 error and throw a not found error.
ELSE if it's another error, throw an error with the status text.
ELSE parse the JSON response and return the image data.

methods.name: handleDeleteImage
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, id: string): Promise<DeleteImageResult>

steps.content: Send a DELETE request to the specific image endpoint using the image ID.
IF the response is not successful, parse the error message and throw an error.
ELSE return a success result.
